prose:
**Aniline Fukui reactivity descriptors** — DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0 (neutral singlet). Cation (+1, doublet) and anion (−1, doublet) also computed at the same level. Geometry: fixed user-supplied coordinates (not re-optimized at DFT). Mulliken population scheme. All three SCF calculations converged; charge conservation checks passed (∑f⁺ = 1.0, ∑f⁻ = 1.0). Most electrophilic atom: C atom 3 (ortho to N), with f⁺ = 2.298. Most nucleophilic atom: N atom 1, with f⁻ = 0.205. Dual descriptor (f⁺ − f⁻) identifies ortho/para carbons as electrophilic sites and N/attached carbons as nucleophilic, consistent with aniline's known reactivity in electrophilic aromatic substitution. Fukui-reactivity visualization saved to `agent_call_01_plot.png` in the output directory. Command: `cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard aniline.xyz`. No warnings. Engine integrity: trustworthy.</prose>
<parameter name="n_atoms">14
